methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 2 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Boom Supersonic
private.rationale: Same sector (Supersonic & Hypersonic), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Venus Aerospace, a Series B frontier-tech company in the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector founded by Sassie and Andrew Duggleby, has raised $44M+ to date. The valuation universe for this stage and sector is limited: two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and two private peers (Boom Supersonic, Hermeus) are identified, though narrowly comparable datapoints meeting same-stage and valuation-tier criteria remain sparse.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable here, using the $44M total raised as a base proxy and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x, yielding an implied valuation of $84M. The Peer-Median approach was not applied due to insufficient comparable density—only two same-sector peers exist, falling below the three-datapoint threshold needed for reliable median triangulation, and broadening the set risked introducing distortion from materially different-stage or tier companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $71M (low) to $96M (high), with a median estimate of $84M. This range reflects applied progression methodology against available stage-based benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the lack of narrow-set peer anchors in the nascent Supersonic & Hypersonic sector and reliance on stage-step multipliers that may not capture sector-specific risk or milestone achievement. Additional comparable data or disclosure of recent fundraising rounds at similar-stage peers would strengthen conviction in this range.
